steps.title: Identify the contrasting relationship
steps.content: The passage presents a contrast: the drummer “did not play drums on the label's classic song "Rapper's Delight"” yet “joined the label after the song's release.” The word “however” must mark this contrast between two independent clauses.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for conjunctive adverbs
steps.content:
Two independent clauses connected by the conjunctive adverb “however” require proper punctuation. A establishes a clear sentence boundary with “, however. He” and effectively separates the two independent clauses into distinct sentences.
  • B is grammatically malformed
  • C creates a comma splice (joining independent clauses with only a comma)
  • D leaves the clauses running together without adequate punctuation


That confirms A is correct.
